channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Australia's prime minister is criticized for buying a waterfront home during a housing crisis
item.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is being criticized for buying a multimillion dollar waterfront home during a national housing crisis with federal elections only months away.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel strikes southern suburbs of Beirut for the first time in nearly a week
item.description: Israeli jets struck the southern suburbs of Beirut early Wednesday for the first time in six days, Lebanese state media reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Zelenskyy to unveil 'victory plan' to Ukrainian lawmakers after presenting it to Western allies
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is set to unveil a blueprint for his country's victory in the war against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In the heartland of Mexico's Sinaloa cartel, the old ways have changed and violence rages
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Cellphone chats have become death sentences in the continuing, bloody factional war inside Mexico's Sinaloa drug cartel. Cartel gunmen stop youths on the street or in their cars and demand their phones.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Set sail on world’s largest cruise ship, which can accommodate over 7,000 guests per voyage
item.description: Icon of the Seas is the world's largest cruise ship. With extensive dining options, record-breaking pools and breathtaking live shows, there's something for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN says Yemen risks being dragged into Mideast conflict that could spiral out of control
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Yemen risks being dragged further into the military escalation in the Middle East that keeps intensifying and could spiral out of control, the U.N. special envoy for the Arab world’s poorest nation said Tuesday. Hans Grundberg told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez fires back at Fetterman’s 'bleak dunk attempt' on Gaza
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) fired back at Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) over his “bleak dunk attempt” on the ongoing war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Mexican president acknowledges army killings of 3 civilians in a violent border city
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico’s new president acknowledged Tuesday that three civilians including a child died in two shootouts involving the military last week in a violent border city.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: In a move that underlines North Korea's growing anger with South Korea, North Korea has blown up sections of unused rail routes and roads that once connected the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian man rescued after 67 days adrift at sea. His family members didn't make it.
item.description: A Russian man was rescued in the Sea of Okhotsk after surviving for two months adrift in an inflatable boat without engine power, but his brother and nephew died.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump on reported Putin calls: 'If I did, it is a smart thing'
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday defended conversations he's reportedly had with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving the White House, even as he declined to confirm they took place.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Russia, China collaborating with criminal networks in cyberattacks against adversaries: Microsoft
item.description: Russia, China and Iran are increasingly collaborating with cybercrime networks to launch a variety of attacks against their adversaries, including the U.S., Microsoft said in a Tuesday report.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: US threatens Israel with arms shipment suspensions over Gaza conditions: Reports
item.description: The Biden administration is telling Israeli officials to take immediate measures to address the humanitarian situation in the Gaza Strip or risk cutting off access to key U.S. military assistance.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A grand museum displaying Egypt's ancient treasures is opening for a trial run
item.description: The long-delayed Grand Egyptian Museum will partially open its main galleries Wednesday, showcasing 12 halls that exhibit aspects of ancient Egypt.
item.dc:creator: Ahmed Hatem, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia releases man whose daughter's drawing opposed Ukraine fighting
item.description: A group that monitors political detentions in Russia says that a man convicted of discrediting the military after his daughter made a drawing criticizing Russia’s military actions in Ukraine has been released from prison after serving 22 months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: America's THAAD anti-missile system starts arriving in Israel as Pentagon says it will be 'fully operational'
item.description: The first components of a U.S. missile defense system aimed at protecting Israel from attacks by Iran have been delivered, according to the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kenya's High Court rejects move to stop deputy president's impeachment debate
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya's High Court on Tuesday rejected an application by the deputy president's lawyers to stop the senate from debating an impeachment motion against him after parliament voted to remove him from office last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southern Africa is enduring its worst hunger crisis in decades due to El Niño, the UN says
item.description: The United Nations' food agency says months of drought in southern Africa triggered by the El Niño weather phenomenon have had a devastating impact on more than 27 million people and caused the region’s worst hunger crisis in decades.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli forces seize documents that reveal Hamas plan for more elaborate attacks: report
item.description: Hundreds of documents seized by Israeli forces reveal a broader ambition by Hamas to plot attacks against Israel in the years before Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada expels India's top diplomat and alleges wider diplomatic involvement in crimes
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada said it has identified India’s top diplomat in the country as a person of interest in the assassination of a Sikh activist there and expelled him and five other diplomats Monday, in an escalating dispute over the June 2023 killing and allegations of other crimes.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, AIJAZ HUSSAIN and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez criticizes Biden administration over 'unrestrained' Israel government
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) criticized the Biden administration on Monday over what she called an “unrestrained” Israeli government.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Pandas en route to National Zoo after a year
item.description: Two new giant pandas are reportedly en route to the National Zoo in Washington, D.C. “Something *giant* is coming to Washington, D.C. via the @FedEx Panda Express!” the zoo said in a post on social media platform X on Monday. “The Zoo will be closed to the public tomorrow, Oct. 15.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: French citizen convicted in Russia of collecting military information and gets 3 years in prison
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday convicted a French citizen of collecting military information and sentenced him to three years in prison. Laurent Vinatier, who was arrested in Moscow in June, earlier admitted guilt, setting the stage for a fast-tracked trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Koreans fighting in Ukraine alongside Russians, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Sunday that North Korean troops are now fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, appearing to confirm reports that have been circulating in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: China surrounds Taiwan in military exercise against independence
item.description: China has launched massive military exercises surrounding Taiwan in response to the island refusing to follow the rule of the Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Maybe advanced civilizations aren’t all that advanced: Scientist
item.description: Antonio Gelis-Filho writes that perhaps advanced civilizations have reached the limits of their technology, and we may be close to doing the same.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Interpol launches campaign to help solve 46 cold cases of women whose bodies were found in Europe
item.description: Interpol launched an initiative last week to seek the public's help in solving 46 cold cases of women whose bodies were discovered in Europe between 1982 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea sends warning to South Korea, saying troops ready to strike if more drones appear
item.description: North Korea said its army units are prepared to launch strikes against South Korea if another drone carrying propaganda flyers flies into the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran making billions in oil revenues under Biden admin as expert slams sanctions policy for lack of 'pressure'
item.description: Some critics have claimed the revenues do not paint an accurate picture due to the fluctuation of prices and lack of reliable sources, but an expert argued that other reports confirm an export surge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indian politician with Bollywood ties shot dead in Mumbai weeks before key state election
item.description: Baba Siddique, 66, a senior politician in India’s financial capital Mumbai, has been fatally shot weeks before a key state election, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Archaeologists unearth well-preserved 4,000-year-old Egyptian tomb
item.description: A joint Egyptian-German archaeological mission found the tomb of an ancient governor's daughter. Two tombs were found, one inside the other, covered in intricate script and art.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9
